claim: B1: 14 minutes CPU training (no GPU required)
reason: Login node arf-ui1 enforces ulimit -t 300s (CPU time). BERTurk forward passes during training exceed this within seconds. Full training requires a SLURM compute job submission.

stored_evidence.STATUS.md:489: Training COMPLETED: CPU job 5506392 on orfoz243, 14 min 40 sec
stored_evidence.STATUS.md:23: Training: 14 minutes on CPU (Orfoz), 30 epochs, batch_size=128, AdamW lr=1e-3

hardware_disclosure: STATUS.md discloses 'orfoz243' which is TRUBA Orfoz cluster (112-core Intel Xeon, no GPU). Audit host arf-ui1 is same cluster family. Hardware match: YES.
documentation_gap: README states '14 minutes on a standard CPU' without disclosing the CPU model. STATUS.md does disclose Orfoz hardware.
verdict_B1: UNVERIFIABLE_LIVE — Cannot re-run training due to CPU time limit. Stored evidence (SLURM job log in STATUS.md) is consistent with the claim. B1 plausible but not independently confirmed.
verdict_B1_code: CONFIRMED at code level — all CUDA references gated by torch.cuda.is_available() or --device defaulting to CPU. The training script can run on CPU-only hardware.
